title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: - High: [canon-build-codex.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-build-codex.sh#L100) falls back to `eval "$task"` outside a git repo. The prompt explicitly says users enter natural-language tasks, so a non-repo pane now interprets arbitrary text as shell. That is command-injection-by-design and will also misbehave on normal prose input. - High: [auto-push-after-commit.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L27) decides commit success by grepping `stdout` for `"error"`/`"fatal"` and then unconditionally runs `git push` if an upstream exists at [line 39](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L39). If `git commit` fails via exit code or `stderr`, this hook can still publish previously unpushed commits, which is a serious unintended external side effect. - Medium: [eval-skill.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L318) removed the old CJK/non-command filtering from bash fenced-block parsing but still treats any remaining non-empty line as a potential binary. Japanese/output/example lines inside ```bash blocks will now be counted as phantom commands, skewing scores and penalties for legitimate skills. Residual risk: I did not find hardcoded secrets in newly added files, but the change set includes a large amount of generated telemetry/log artifacts (`experience.jsonl`, `golden-store`, `correction-pairs`) that look commit-noisy and potentially privacy-sensitive if they are meant to stay local.
